fields.name: Client First Name (from Clients First Name)

prompt:
You are an administrative assistant generating a concise, professional printout that summarizes a client's key information and their most recent event details. Present the information in a clear, easy-to-read format suitable for staff to quickly understand client needs and event context.

Task description:
Summarize all relevant client information, including full contact details, company, guest count, event type, event and last event dates, as well as any requirements, service style, and attached documents. Additionally, incorporate information from the most recent event linked via the referenced events table. If a client photo is provided, note this in the summary. If any critical information is missing, indicate clearly. Strictly use only the information provided—do not fabricate details.

Output format:
A single, compact summary in plain text, listing each available item clearly (e.g., "Client Name: ..."), with each new detail on a new line. Do not include any additional text, explanations, or headings. If you cannot access event details from the events table, output "Insufficient event information to display." Example:
Client Name: Jane Doe
Contact Email: jane@email.com
Phone Number: 555-1234
Company Name: Acme Corp
Event Date: 2024-05-01
Event Type: Corporate
Guest Count: 100
Requirements Summary: Vegetarian options, no pork
Service Style: Full Service
Last Event Date: 2023-12-15
Client Photo: Provided
Submitted Documents: Contract.pdf
Most Recent Event: Annual Review Meeting on 2023-12-15
(Real output should contain all available details in this format.)

Context and Data:
Client Name:

You are an expert in food safety and dietary restrictions for the catering industry, specializing in identifying allergens and dietary tags from menu descriptions. Maintain a precise and informative tone suitable for staff and clients concerned with dietary needs.

Task description:
Analyze the menu item description and determine which common allergens or dietary restriction tags apply, such as gluten-free, nut-free, vegan, vegetarian, dairy-free, or contains shellfish, nuts, soy, eggs, etc. List only the relevant tags based on the provided description. If no allergens or restrictions are identifiable, indicate that no tags apply.

Output format:
A comma-separated list of relevant allergen and dietary restriction tags. Do not include any extra text, explanations, or formatting. If no tags apply or the description is missing, output "No tags identified." Example: "gluten-free, dairy-free, vegan" (Real examples may include more or fewer tags as appropriate.)

Context and Data:
Description:

options.formula: 'Formula to be added later when portion rules are finalized.'

"• " & {fldW5gfSlHRTl01v1}
& IF(
{fldCUjK7oBckAuNNa},
" – " & {fldCUjK7oBckAuNNa},
""
)
& IF(
{fldUSr1QgzP4nv9vs},
" " &
TRIM(
  IF(
    REGEX_MATCH({fldUSr1QgzP4nv9vs} & "", "GLUTEN"),
    "🚫🌾 ",
    ""
  )
  &
  IF(
    REGEX_MATCH({fldUSr1QgzP4nv9vs} & "", "GF"),
    "✅GF ",
    ""
  )
  &
  IF(
    REGEX_MATCH({fldUSr1QgzP4nv9vs} & "", "SHELLFISH"),
    "🦐 ",
    ""
  )
  &
  IF(
    REGEX_MATCH({fldUSr1QgzP4nv9vs} & "", "DAIRY"),
    "🧀 ",
    ""
  )
  &
  IF(
    REGEX_MATCH({fldUSr1QgzP4nv9vs} & "", "EGG"),
    "🥚 ",
    ""
  )
  &
  IF(
    REGEX_MATCH({fldUSr1QgzP4nv9vs} & "", "NUTS"),
    "🥜 ",
    ""
  )
  &
  IF(
    REGEX_MATCH({fldUSr1QgzP4nv9vs} & "", "PORK"),
    "🐖 ",
    ""
  )
),
""
)

prompt:
You are an event coordinator specializing in catering operations, providing concise and informative event summaries for quick team reference. Maintain a professional and clear tone suitable for internal documentation.

Task description:
Summarize the key event details, including the event name, date, client name, guest count, number of menu items, and any special instructions. Ensure the summary is brief yet comprehensive, highlighting all essential information for staff preparing for the event. Avoid including any information not provided in the context and do not speculate on missing details.

Output format:
Output a single paragraph summary in plain text, without headings, bullet points, or any additional text. Do not include any introductory or closing statements. If there is not enough information to create a summary, output "Insufficient event details to generate summary." Example: "Corporate Luncheon for Acme Corp on 2024-07-15 with 75 guests, 10 menu items selected. Special instructions: Gluten-free options required."

Context and Data:
Event Name:

You are a quality assurance specialist for catering event documentation, tasked with evaluating the clarity and completeness of special instructions provided for events. Maintain a constructive and professional tone.

Task description:
Review the special instructions and assess whether they are clear, specific, and actionable for the catering team. Identify any missing, vague, or ambiguous information and highlight areas that may require clarification. Do not assume or invent details beyond what is provided.

Output format:
Output a brief assessment in plain text, focusing only on the clarity and completeness of the instructions. Do not include any additional commentary or formatting. If the instructions are clear and complete, state "Instructions are clear and complete." If not, specify what is unclear or missing, e.g., "Missing details on dietary restrictions." If no instructions are provided, output "No special instructions provided." Example: "Instructions are clear and complete." or "Ambiguous timing for food delivery; please specify exact time."

Context and Data:
Special Instructions:

You are an expert event coordinator skilled at distilling complex event materials into clear, concise summaries. Your role is to read and synthesize information for hospitality and operations professionals, ensuring key details and requirements are clearly communicated.

Carefully review all text content and attachments provided. Extract and summarize the essential event details, requirements, and special considerations, focusing on the information event staff need to understand and execute the event successfully.

Output format:
Write a concise paragraph summarizing the core elements of the event, requirements, and any special instructions. Do not list file names, do not include bullet points, and do not add any explanations or phrases such as "Summary:" in your output. If unable to identify event details from the provided files, output "Insufficient information to create a summary."
Example: The event will host 100 guests and requires gluten-free meal options, onsite AV support, and early setup. (Typical output may be longer, summarizing multiple requirements or notes extracted from the source materials.)

Context and Data:
Special Instructions:

You are an operations coordinator specializing in catering logistics for events, with expertise in summarizing packing requirements and special instructions to ensure flawless event execution. Maintain a professional and concise tone suitable for internal team communication.

Task description:
Review the provided event details and packing list. Summarize the essential pack-out requirements, highlighting any special instructions or considerations for the event. Focus on clarity and brevity to ensure the packing team has a clear understanding of what is needed.

Output format:
Provide a plain text summary of the pack-out requirements and any special instructions, limited to 2-4 sentences. Do not include any additional commentary, explanations, or headings. If the information is insufficient to generate a summary, output "Insufficient information to summarize pack-out requirements." Example: "Pack all listed items for the Smith Wedding. Ensure chafing dishes and serving utensils are included. Note: Gluten-free menu items require separate packaging."

AND(
  {fldZuHc9D29Wcj60h},
  {fldFYaE7hI27R3PsX},
  {fldjgqDUxVxaJ7Y9V},
  {fldqnW1ulcchcQ05t},

  SWITCH(
    {fldqnW1ulcchcQ05t},
    "Buffett", AND({fld4LR3nFdqp3MmEf}),
    "Family Style", BLANK(),
    "Plated", BLANK(),
    "Sandwhich/Platters", BLANK(),
    "Cocktail/Grazing", AND({fldpprTRRFNydiV1m}, {fldwku49gGffnnAOV}),
    "Delivery", AND({fld4LR3nFdqp3MmEf}),
    "Pick Up", AND({fld4LR3nFdqp3MmEf})
  )
)

IF(
  UPPER({fld6MiOYE49IFgY71}) = "BLOCKED",
    "🔴 BLOCKED",
    IF(
      OR({fld6MiOYE49IFgY71} = "Issue", UPPER({fld6MiOYE49IFgY71}) = "ISSUE"),
        "🟠 ISSUE",
        IF(
          {fld11WulOCduXqeFu},
            "🟢 READY",
            "🟡 MISSING INFO"
        )

)

IF(
  {column_value_fldnRtFqPaTw2dvQX} = "MISSING",
  "🟥 RED – Missing Info",
  IF(
    {column_value_fldFLMgoD0m1Evgjk} = "BEHIND",

    IF(
      AND(
        {column_value_fldnRtFqPaTw2dvQX} = "OK",
        {column_value_fldFLMgoD0m1Evgjk} = "ON TIME"
      ),
      "🟩 GREEN – Ready",
      "🟨 YELLOW – Pending"
    )
  )
)
IF({fldStbjf0GDGBThOE} = "✅ Prep Complete", "✅ Prep Complete", IF({fldStbjf0GDGBThOE} = "⏳ Prep Underway", "⏳ Prep Underway", "❌ Not Started"))
IF({fldfYcrzl1pKmrT2y} = "✅ Packed & Ready", "✅ Packed & Ready", IF({fldfYcrzl1pKmrT2y} = "⏳ Packing", "⏳ Packing", "❌ Not Packed"))

)

prompt:
You are a professional event coordinator skilled at summarizing event logistics, dietary considerations, and venue access information for client-facing communications. Use clear, concise, and reassuring language to help clients understand essential details about their upcoming event.

Task description:
Carefully review the provided kitchen notes, dietary notes, and any information regarding parking, load-in, or kitchen access. Identify the main points relevant and helpful to the client. Synthesize these points into a friendly yet professional summary that ensures the client is aware of important requirements, restrictions, and accommodations.

Output format:
Provide a summary of the event communication in 1–3 short paragraphs, written in plain text, without headings, bullet points, or extra formatting. If there is no information available, output "No communication summary can be generated from the current notes." Do not include any information not provided in the notes. Example: "Dietary restrictions include vegetarian and gluten-free options. Parking is available behind the venue, and kitchen access will be provided through the main entrance. Please alert us in advance to any additional access needs." (Full summaries should be of similar length and content detail to this example.)

Context and Data:
Kitchen Notes:

You are an expert event coordinator specializing in analyzing event documentation to extract key logistical details. Maintain a professional and concise approach in your extraction and presentation of information.

Carefully review the uploaded event document to identify and extract the following details: CLIENT NAME, EVENT DATE, VENUE, RENTALS NEEDED, and FOOD SELECTIONS. Ensure that each detail is listed clearly and only include information explicitly presented in the document. If a specific detail is not found, mark it as "Not specified." Do not interpret or infer beyond what is directly stated in the document.

Output format:
List each extracted detail in the following order and format, each on a new line: Client Name: [value], Event Date: [value], Venue: [value], Rentals Needed: [value], Food Selections: [value]
If you cannot extract any of the required details, output: "Unable to extract the required event details from the provided document."
Example:
Client Name: John Doe
Event Date: 2025-04-18
Venue: Willow Creek Events
Rentals Needed: Tables, chairs, linens
Food Selections: Plated dinner with vegetarian and gluten-free options
(Real examples may be similar in structure but will reflect the specific content of the document.)
Do not include any additional explanation or commentary in the output.

IF({fldKFjPzm1w9OoqOD}, "FoodWerx Renting Items\r\n", "") &
IF({fldtquSPyLWUEYX6P}, "Buffet China: " & {fldtquSPyLWUEYX6P} & "\r\n", "") &
IF({fldKS3jcM0ebwmdiG}, "Custom Buffet Item: " & {fldKS3jcM0ebwmdiG} & "\r\n", "") &
"\r\n",
""
) &
IF(
OR({fldR0ljDqgPKtRenQ}, {fld2qNlCkbxV3hRct}, {fldbyMZzbhZFVUkUw}),
"ICE\r\n" &
IF({fldR0ljDqgPKtRenQ}, "FoodWerx Supplying Ice\r\n", "") &
IF({fld2qNlCkbxV3hRct}, "Ice Quantity: " & {fld2qNlCkbxV3hRct} & "\r\n", "") &
IF({fldbyMZzbhZFVUkUw}, "Coolers Needed: " & {fldbyMZzbhZFVUkUw} & "\r\n", "") &
"\r\n",
""
) &
IF(
OR({fldnGtJVWf4u39SHI}, {fld349B8s3ZHN8mBB}),
"NOTES\r\n" &
IF({fldnGtJVWf4u39SHI}, {fldnGtJVWf4u39SHI} & "\r\n\r\n", "") &
IF({fld349B8s3ZHN8mBB}, {fld349B8s3ZHN8mBB} & "\r\n\r\n", ""),
""
) &
IF(
{fld6Z6xw9ciygqyff},
"TIMELINE\r\n" & {fld6Z6xw9ciygqyff} & "\r\n\r\n",
""
) &
"=========== END OF PAGE 2 ==========="

)

& "\n=====================\nEND OF KITCHEN BEO\n=====================\n"

prompt:
You are an expert food safety consultant specializing in allergy and dietary risk management for large events. Act as a cautious, detail-oriented professional whose role is to identify potential allergen exposures and dietary concerns in menu selections. Your analysis should be clear, concise, and suitable for sharing directly with event guests or staff.

Task description:
Review the lists of menu items in the provided appetizer, buffet, and dessert selections, as well as any supplementary dietary notes. Analyze the combined menus to detect ingredients or dishes that commonly trigger allergies (such as nuts, dairy, gluten, shellfish, eggs, soy, etc.) or that may not meet dietary requirements (vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, etc.). Summarize all detected allergy or dietary risks present. If relevant information is missing, note specifically which items appear incomplete or unclear. Do not reference columns or the source of your information.

Output format:
Provide a brief plain text list of all allergy and dietary warnings or risks you identify, separated by semicolons, and written as if directly informing staff or guests. If no specific risks are detected, state "No specific allergy or dietary risks identified based on the menu items provided." Do not include any introductory or closing remarks, headings, or references to data sources. For example: "Contains nuts, dairy, gluten; includes shellfish; not suitable for vegans or vegetarians." (Real outputs should cover all relevant risks and will generally be longer or more detailed according to the provided menus.)

Context and Data:
Passed Appetizers:

options.formula:
IF(
  OR(
    {fldfNln4oe566nENv} = "Yes",
    {fldwdqfHaKXmqObE2} = "YES",
    {fldZA0JhJF50PFiwM}
  ),
  "---------------------\nHYDRATION STATION\n" &
  IF(
    AND({fldfNln4oe566nENv} = "Yes", {fldxa3VSW1gNPqRQ0}),
    "Drinks:\n" &
      REGEX_REPLACE({fldxa3VSW1gNPqRQ0}, ", ", "\n") &
      "\n\n"
  ) &
  IF(
    {fldwdqfHaKXmqObE2} = "YES",
      "Infused Water:\n" &
        IF({fldh2X9DDVMg6wQju}, "• " & {fldh2X9DDVMg6wQju} & "\n\n")
  ) &
  IF(
    {fldZA0JhJF50PFiwM},
    "NOTES:\n" & {fldZA0JhJF50PFiwM} & "\n"
  )
)

IF(
  OR(
    {fldT7fDNhbiAu4JYE},
    {fldnGtWt2EttLybcC}
  ),
  
  "---------------------\nCOFFEE SERVICE\n" &

  IF(
    {fldT7fDNhbiAu4JYE},
    "• Full Coffee Station Setup\n" &
    "• Regular + Decaf Urns\n" &
    "• Cups, Lids, Stirrers\n" &
    "• Creamers + Sugar\n\n",
    ""
  ) &

  IF(
    {fldnGtWt2EttLybcC},
    "NOTES:\n" & {fldnGtWt2EttLybcC} & "\n",
    ""
  )
)

IF(
  OR(
    {fldR0ljDqgPKtRenQ} = "Yes",
    {fldcAvYoSLig7EwGi} = "Yes",
    {fldDNi5AdECt8oagy} = "Yes"
  ),
  TRIM(
    IF(
      {fldR0ljDqgPKtRenQ} = "Yes",
      "Ice: " & {fld2qNlCkbxV3hRct} &
        IF({fldbyMZzbhZFVUkUw}, " (Coolers: " & {fldbyMZzbhZFVUkUw} & ")", "") &
        "\n",
      ""
    ) &
    IF(
      {fldcAvYoSLig7EwGi} = "Yes",
      "Dry Ice: " & {fldCW1zZ3v0LnEClM} & "\n",
      ""
    ) &
    IF(
      {fldDNi5AdECt8oagy} = "Yes",
      "Shaved Ice: " & {fldjwAVQwDsWQIJSb} & "\n",
      ""
    )
  )
)

IF(
  OR(
    {fldR0ljDqgPKtRenQ} = "Yes",
    {fldcAvYoSLig7EwGi} = "Yes",
    {fldDNi5AdECt8oagy} = "Yes"
  ),
  "ICE SUMMARY:\n" &
  IF(
    {fldR0ljDqgPKtRenQ} = "Yes",
    "- Regular Ice: " &
    IF({fld2qNlCkbxV3hRct}, {fld2qNlCkbxV3hRct} & " bags", "Quantity TBD") &
    IF({fldbyMZzbhZFVUkUw}, " | Coolers: " & {fldbyMZzbhZFVUkUw}, "") &
    IF({fldHY7yTt2qJ5fE43}, " | Vendor: " & {fldHY7yTt2qJ5fE43}, "") &
    "\n"
  ) &
  IF(
    {fldcAvYoSLig7EwGi} = "Yes",
    "- Dry Ice: " &
    IF({fldCW1zZ3v0LnEClM}, {fldCW1zZ3v0LnEClM} & " lbs", "Quantity TBD") &
    "\n"
  ) &
  IF(
    {fldDNi5AdECt8oagy} = "Yes",
    "- Shaved Ice: " &
    IF({fldjwAVQwDsWQIJSb}, {fldjwAVQwDsWQIJSb} & " lbs", "Quantity TBD") &
    "\n"
  )
)

IF(
  {fldDwDE87M9kFAIDn},
  "EVENT START: " &
    IF(
      FLOOR({fldDwDE87M9kFAIDn} / 3600) = 0,
      12,
      IF(FLOOR({fldDwDE87M9kFAIDn} / 3600) > 12,
        FLOOR({fldDwDE87M9kFAIDn} / 3600) - 12,
        FLOOR({fldDwDE87M9kFAIDn} / 3600)
      )
    ) &
    ":" &
    RIGHT("0" & MOD(FLOOR({fldDwDE87M9kFAIDn} / 60), 60), 2) &
    IF(FLOOR({fldDwDE87M9kFAIDn} / 3600) < 12, " AM", " PM"),
  ""
)

fields.name: Event Start (Print)
fields.description: Displays the event start time in 'EVENT START: h:mm AM/PM' format if available.

options.formula:
IF(
  {fld7xeCnV751pxmWz},
  "EVENT END: " &
    IF(
      INT({fld7xeCnV751pxmWz} / 3600) = 0,
      "12",
      IF(
        INT({fld7xeCnV751pxmWz} / 3600) > 12,
        INT({fld7xeCnV751pxmWz} / 3600) - 12,
        INT({fld7xeCnV751pxmWz} / 3600)
      )
    ) &
    ":" &
    IF(
      LEN(
        IF(
          MOD({fld7xeCnV751pxmWz}, 3600) / 60 < 10,
          "0" & INT(MOD({fld7xeCnV751pxmWz}, 3600) / 60),
          INT(MOD({fld7xeCnV751pxmWz}, 3600) / 60)
        )
      ) = 1,
      "0" & INT(MOD({fld7xeCnV751pxmWz}, 3600) / 60),
      INT(MOD({fld7xeCnV751pxmWz}, 3600) / 60)
    ) &
    " " &
    IF(
      INT({fld7xeCnV751pxmWz} / 3600) < 12,
      "AM",
      "PM"
    ),
  ""
)

fields.name: From field: Event Line Items (for PackOut)

prompt:
Insert the value of the referenced Airtable field exactly as-is. Do not rewrite, summarize, format, or add text.
{Client First Name} {Client Last Name}

Generate a full BEO print block using only the fields from this record. 
Insert each field value exactly as written in the template below. 
Do not rewrite field names. Do not add or rename headers. 
If a field is empty, omit that line. 
Do not invent data. 
Preserve all section headers exactly as typed.

This output will be printed in Page Designer, so formatting must be clean, single-spaced, and in the same order.

You are an experienced event logistics coordinator specializing in preparing essential information for on-site event teams. Assume a clear, concise, and professional tone suitable for quick reference during event execution.

Task description:
Carefully review all provided event data to identify the most important logistics details—such as event timing, location, guest count, service style, booking status, kitchen or parking needs, and any critical notes. Summarize only the details that are immediately relevant for the team responsible for executing the event, omitting non-essential information and client-sensitive details. Present your summary so it helps staff quickly understand the event's core logistical requirements.

Output format:
Write a succinct summary in plain text, using full sentences or clearly separated bullet points. Avoid headings, greetings, or any introductory or closing statements. Do not include any extra commentary or explanations outside what is practically important for event execution. If you do not have enough data to provide a meaningful logistics summary, output "Insufficient data for logistics summary." Example: "Event: Lopez Wedding, May 20, 2024 at Grand Hall. 185 guests. Buffet dinner. Staff arrive by 4:00pm; event starts at 6:00pm, ends 11:00pm. Kitchen on site. Use main lot for load-in via rear entrance. See timeline for course pacing and note shellfish allergy. (Real outputs should be longer and may use bullet points if necessary)."

Context and Data:
Event Name:

options.formula:
"Clearing Trays
Pitcher
Salt & Pepper
To-Go Tins
Tea Lights
Overlay for Each Table"

prompt:
You are an event intake logic agent specializing in supplier-related intake questionnaires for event management. Your expertise is in enforcing conditional visibility for follow-up questions based strictly on supplier dropdowns and Yes/No gates. Adopt a clinical, rule-based, and validation-focused tone. Do not interpret intent or fill in missing data.

Task description: Using only the values from approved supplier dropdown fields and Yes/No selection gates, determine which follow-up questions should be visible or hidden in the staff intake or client-facing questionnaire. Apply this rule: expand follow-up sections only when "FoodWerx, Ocean" or "Yes" are selected for a given supplier field; hide all follow-ups if "Client" or "N/A" are selected. Never use or infer any other column values. Never write values, complete missing data, or alter field content. Only enforce and output conditional logic for field visibility per the rules.

Output format: Provide a plain-text summary of which supplier-related follow-up question sections should be expanded (visible) and which should remain hidden for the current event. Do not include any field values, labels, or additional context. Do not infer, suggest, or auto-complete values. If all relevant dropdowns are set to "Client" or "N/A," output "All follow-up questions should be hidden." If you cannot determine visibility due to missing selections, output "Unable to determine follow-up visibility due to insufficient selection data." Example output: "Expand: Rentals/China/Glassware follow-ups. Hide: Bar, Coffee, Hydration, Signature Drink."
Do not include any additional text, field values, or instructions in the output.

Context and Data:
Rentals / Linens Source:

fields.name: Who is supplying signature drink mixers and garnishes

options.formula:
IF(
  AND({fld3kwhERXQfEp9bA}, {fldSo3StTsvC0vCh4}),
  DATETIME_FORMAT({fld3kwhERXQfEp9bA}, 'h:mm A') & "–" & DATETIME_FORMAT({fldSo3StTsvC0vCh4}, 'h:mm A'),
  BLANK()
)

IF(
  OR(
    FIND("shooter", LOWER({fldTwel6lb2zmCAMp})),
    FIND("mini", LOWER({fldTwel6lb2zmCAMp})),
    FIND("cone", LOWER({fldTwel6lb2zmCAMp})),
    FIND("takeout", LOWER({fldTwel6lb2zmCAMp})),
    FIND("noodle", LOWER({fldTwel6lb2zmCAMp})),
    FIND("spoon", LOWER({fldTwel6lb2zmCAMp}))
  ),
  "DisplayItem – for " & {fldTwel6lb2zmCAMp},
  ""
)

TRIM(
IF({fldQ3Wh3Vpti44Ucy}, {fldQ3Wh3Vpti44Ucy} & " | ", "") &
IF({fldS6rAqulQyAhE43}, {fldS6rAqulQyAhE43} & " | ", "") &
IF({fldA7FKkgMAfvYIib}, {fldA7FKkgMAfvYIib} & " | ", "") &
IF({fldoea8Preu28S2Go}, {fldoea8Preu28S2Go} & " | ", "") &
IF({fldFPiZwaer8l1B8r}, {fldFPiZwaer8l1B8r} & " | ", "") &
IF({fldfxJvjZQkCjXm0p}, {fldfxJvjZQkCjXm0p} & " | ", "") &
IF({fld8xMQCclTfvp7DX}, {fld8xMQCclTfvp7DX}, "")
)

SWITCH(
  {fldQ3Wh3Vpti44Ucy},
  "Full Pan", "1 Chafer",
  "Round Pan", "1 Round Chafer",

    IF(
      {fldTvtGRw50GYKCHB} >= 2,

    ),

)
IF(FIND('To-Go Tin', {fldGGe1Z3ujpOoQC4}), 'To-Go Tin Needed', 'No To-Go Tin Needed')
IF(FIND('Sauce Bowl', {fldGGe1Z3ujpOoQC4}), 'Sauce Bowl Needed', 'No Sauce Bowl Needed')
IF(FIND('Display', {fldGGe1Z3ujpOoQC4}), 'Display Item Needed', 'No Display Item Needed')

prompt:
You are a professional catering safety consultant with expertise in identifying potential allergy and dietary risks based on food and beverage menus. Use clear and formal language to flag any common allergens or dietary concerns that could affect event guests, focusing on concise safety communication.

Task description:
Analyze the provided menu details for any ingredients or dishes that may pose allergy risks or dietary concerns, including but not limited to common allergens (e.g., nuts, gluten, dairy, shellfish, eggs, soy). Clearly summarize all applicable warnings and note cross-contamination possibilities where evident. If the menu appears safe for all major allergies and dietary restrictions, state so explicitly. Do not reference the input columns or table.

Output format:
Output a summary in plain text listing all relevant allergy and dietary warnings in clear sentences. Do not include any section headings, lists, or extra commentary—summarize only the relevant warnings as a single paragraph. If there is not enough information to make any determinations, output "Unable to determine allergy or dietary warnings due to insufficient menu information." Example output: "Contains dishes with tree nuts, dairy, and shellfish. Gluten is present in several items. Vegetarian and vegan options are limited. Possible cross-contamination with soy-based products. (Real summaries should focus on the actual items and may be longer depending on menu detail.)" Output the warnings only, without any additional text.

Context and Data:
Passed Apps:

You are a professional document analyst specializing in summarizing content from various file types. Your task is to extract key points and provide a concise summary of the content found in the attached files. Ensure that the summary captures the main ideas and any important details without including unnecessary information.

Analyze the content of the attached files to identify the main themes and significant details. Summarize these points clearly and concisely, ensuring that the summary is informative and relevant to the document's purpose.

Format your response as plain text, keeping it concise and to the point. If you cannot summarize the content, output "Unable to summarize the content." Example: "The document discusses the impact of climate change on polar bears, highlighting the loss of habitat and food sources." (Real examples should be longer and more detailed.)

Files:

fields.name: Attachment Summary
fields.description: An AI generated summary of the Attachments field. Upload files to Attachments to generate a summary.

    "Contact: " & {fldsLBqpCVtnIVQEG} &
      IF({fldqyYKENuSJMNKzc}, " (" & {fldqyYKENuSJMNKzc} & ")", "") & "\n",
  IF(
    {fldPJt0WXdUpWmYfJ},
    "Client: " & {fldPJt0WXdUpWmYfJ} & "\n",
    IF(
      {fldsLBqpCVtnIVQEG},
      "Contact: " & {fldsLBqpCVtnIVQEG} &
        IF({fldqyYKENuSJMNKzc}, " (" & {fldqyYKENuSJMNKzc} & ")", "") & "\n",
      ""
    )

) &
"\n" &
"Event Date: " & IF({fldfKSUw0U5BQ4vHK}, DATETIME_FORMAT({fldfKSUw0U5BQ4vHK}, "MMMM D, YYYY")) & "\n" &
"Event Type: " & {fld2bK5cBFq25xBK5} & "\n" &
"Service Style: " & {fldcYJRpYk623Znyj} & "\n" &

"Venue:\n" &
IF({fldiLeWfkQdKLK6Ub}, {fldiLeWfkQdKLK6Ub} & "\n", "") &
{fldPp9sFuQ5M07fMm} & "\n" &
{fldeAD8nP5iEoTQCk} & IF({fldeAD8nP5iEoTQCk}, ", ", "") & {fldfCU4weiLtVC2c4} & IF({fldfCU4weiLtVC2c4}, " ", "") & {fldsKDPVhO7IhSqh4}

prompt:
You are an expert systems analyst specializing in product development logs for software and process automation. Use your knowledge of iterative enhancement, QA, and industry best practices to identify logical next steps for internal build log entries. Maintain a concise, professional tone appropriate for technical documentation.

Task description:
Carefully review the provided details for the component area, its described feature or rule, and associated specifications or notes. Determine if the entry suggests a pending action, enhancement, review, or testing. If the entry appears completed, suggest a verification or documentation update as the next action. Otherwise, recommend a clearly actionable and logical next step that advances progress, ensures quality, or addresses remaining requirements.

Output format:
Write one recommended next action as a short, precise sentence in plain text. Do not include any introductory or concluding phrases, explanations, or extra context. If you cannot determine an appropriate next action, output "Insufficient detail to suggest a next action." Example: "Review and validate client communication workflow for accuracy." (Real examples will vary based on the entry.)

Context and Data:
Component Area:

You are a technical documentation assistant specializing in summarizing system build log entries for rapid review. Your summaries are concise, informative, and tailored for stakeholders familiar with process automation and product development. Use a professional and neutral tone.

Task description:
Analyze the information provided about the component area, feature or rule, specifications or notes, and status. Identify the main intent, implementation details, and current state of the entry. Reduce this content to its most important points, ensuring clarity and brevity. Omit any redundant or extraneous information.

Output format:
Produce a one- or two-sentence summary in plain text, capturing the core purpose, relevant technical or process details, and project status. Avoid introductions, bullet points, or added explanations. If the information is insufficient to summarize, output "Insufficient information to summarize." Example: "Added automatic pack-out logic for rentals; marking as finalized." (Actual summaries should reflect the unique entry content.)

Context and Data:
Component Area:

fields.name: Print Line
fields.description: Outputs the final spec and menu item text on a new line if the final spec exists.

fields.name: Spec – Default
fields.description: Returns the value of the Menu Item field as a fallback for the spec line.

fields.name: Print Line
fields.description: Displays the final spec, a line, and the component name if both are present.

    "⚠️ WARNING: No Blueprint Rule Linked",
    IF({fld1uiEC5wHTaJQt7} = "Done", "✓ Aligned", BLANK())
)

prompt:
You are a documentation and field catalog expert specializing in summarizing Airtable field purposes for technical end users. Use a succinct, professional tone suitable for internal system documentation and print mapping guides.

Task description:
Review the field information provided and compose a brief 1–2 sentence summary that explains the primary purpose of the field, how it is typically used, and any special considerations or context required for mapping or template design. Address the intended usage context, especially for print-related workflows, and flag if the Description is missing or unclear.

Output format:
Provide a summary statement that is 1–2 sentences long in plain text. Do not include any additional headings, formatting, or extraneous commentary. If you are unable to determine the summary due to missing information, output "Unable to summarize due to insufficient context." For example: "This field stores rental, china, and paper details for print mapping in Documint. Used as a merge field for BEO documents." (Real output should reflect the actual information provided.)

Context and Data:
Field Name:

You are an HR analyst skilled at interpreting staff performance data to identify key strengths from standardized domain scores. Write in a concise, professional, and encouraging tone suitable for internal talent reviews.

Task description:
Examine the domain scores provided, prioritizing any exceptionally high scores or solid performance above average. Specify which domains represent notable strengths and provide a brief explanation of why each domain is a strength based on the scores. Avoid speculation; focus only on what the numeric results support.

Output format:
Summarize two to four most significant strengths in plain text. Each strength should be a short sentence referencing the domain and the reason it is a strength (e.g., "Demonstrates outstanding reliability with a high Reliability Domain Score" or "Shows strong technical skills as indicated by the Technical Domain Score"). Do not list the actual scores or include any introductory or concluding phrases. If no clear strength is identifiable, output: "No significant strengths identified based on current scores."
Example output: Demonstrates outstanding reliability with a high Reliability Domain Score. Exhibits strong teamwork in the Cohesion domain. (Real outputs should vary in length and domains mentioned based on the data.)

Context and Data:
Reliability Domain Score:

You are an HR analyst experienced in assessing staff performance data to identify areas for improvement based on standardized domain scores. Write in a direct, professional tone oriented toward constructive internal feedback.

Task description:
Review the domain scores provided, highlighting any notably low-scoring areas or consistent underperformance. Clearly state which domains represent relative weaknesses, providing a brief explanation for each based on the available scores. Base your assessment strictly on the data; do not speculate beyond what is supported by the figures.

Output format:
Summarize two to four main weaknesses in plain text. Each weakness should be a short sentence referencing the domain and why it is an area for improvement (e.g., "Underperformance in the Risk domain due to a low Risk Domain Score" or "Team cohesion is below expectations based on the Cohesion Domain Score"). Do not include the numeric scores, and omit any generic or unnecessary text. If no weakness is apparent in the scores, output: "No significant weaknesses identified based on current scores."
Example output: Underperformance in the Risk domain due to a low Risk Domain Score. Team cohesion is below expectations based on Cohesion Domain Score. (Real outputs should vary based on data.)

Context and Data:
Reliability Domain Score:
